text:
h1. Purpose
Provide consistent external communications during outages and failovers. These templates are written to be accurate even when details are still emerging.

h1. Principles
- Acknowledge impact and symptoms (errors/latency).
- Avoid speculation about root cause.
- State what we are doing next (investigating, mitigating, failing over).
- Provide the next update time.

h1. Status page template: incident start
*Title:* Increased error rates for Redwood Hosted API
*Body:* We are investigating elevated error rates and latency affecting a subset of requests to the Redwood Hosted API. We have identified a regional service degradation and are working to mitigate. Next update in 30 minutes.

h1. Status page template: failover initiated
*Title:* Regional degradation; traffic failover in progress
*Body:* We are performing a controlled traffic failover to a secondary region to mitigate o...

title: Postmortem: No free GPUs in us-east caused elevated 503s (2025-12-18)
preview:

On 2025-12-18, us-east experienced a period with effectively zero free GPU slots across the primary hosted pool. Admission control rejected requests with 503 "no capacity" and queue wait times climbed for accepted requests.

h2. Customer impact
* Time window: 18:2219:05 ET
* Symptoms: increased 503s, lon...
text:
h2. Summary
On 2025-12-18, us-east experienced a period with effectively zero free GPU slots across the primary hosted pool. Admission control rejected requests with 503 "no capacity" and queue wait times climbed for accepted requests.

h2. Customer impact
* Time window: 18:2219:05 ET
* Symptoms: increased 503s, long queue wait time, degraded p95 latency
* Affected: hosted API (us-east); spillover to eu-west increased p99 latency there

h2. Detection
* Alert: "gpu_pool_free_slots == 0"
* Status page internal note opened by oncall

h2. Root cause
A region-level routing rule intended for a planned maintenance window remained enabled after the window, preventing cross-region overflow early in the event.

h2. Resolution
* Disabled the stale routing rule
* Increased cross-region spillover budget for hosted tier for 24 hours
* Added temporary manual capacity for peak

h2. Action items
1) Ad...

title: Post-incident summary template (publishable)
preview:
## Purpose
This template is used to produce **customer-publishable** post-incident summaries for Redwood’s public status page and linked surfaces (e.g., Console). The goal is to provide a timely, accurate account of:
- **Customer impact** (what customers experienced)

text:
## Purpose
This template is used to produce **customer-publishable** post-incident summaries for Redwood’s public status page and linked surfaces (e.g., Console). The goal is to provide a timely, accurate account of:
- **Customer impact** (what customers experienced)
- **Timeline** (when it started, when it was mitigated, when it was fully resolved)
- **Root cause class** (what kind of failure occurred, without leaking sensitive details)
- **Mitigations and prevention** (what we did immediately and what we are changing)

This template is designed to be:
- Clear to a non-Redwood audience
- Consistent across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private messaging
- Safe to publish (see confidentiality rules below)

---

## When to publish
Use this summary when an incident is customer-visible and any of the following are true:
- Severity is **Sev0–Sev2** (or equivalent) OR
- The incident resulted in a ...

title: KMS Outage Game Day — Plan + Results (2025 Q4)
preview:
## Summary
This game day exercised Redwood’s KMS dependency under controlled failure conditions (hard outage + throttling + permission misconfig) to validate:
- KMS client wrapper behavior (timeouts, retries, jitter, error taxonomy)
- Circuit breaker + degrade-mode policy to prevent retry storms / cascading failures...
text:
## Summary
This game day exercised Redwood’s KMS dependency under controlled failure conditions (hard outage + throttling + permission misconfig) to validate:
- KMS client wrapper behavior (timeouts, retries, jitter, error taxonomy)
- Circuit breaker + degrade-mode policy to prevent retry storms / cascading failures
- Observability (dashboards + alerts + traces) and on-call runbook readiness
- Customer-facing blast radius assumptions for Hosted/Dedicated/Private deployments

We ran the scenarios in staging and a dedicated canary slice (internal traffic only). We did **not** run fault injection in production.

**High-level outcome:** Core availability targets were mostly met for new requests, but we observed elevated tail latency during partial KMS degradation and two operator-experience gaps (noisy alerts + unclear next steps for AccessDenied). Follow-up work was created and is tracke...

text:
## Purpose
This runbook describes how to post and maintain **customer-facing** status page updates during incidents at Redwood Inference. It is optimized for:
- **Component-level accuracy** (product surface × region × capability)
- **Fast time-to-first-update (TTFU)** with consistent language
- **Verification and anti-flap behavior** (avoid noisy toggling)
- Clear coordination across **#incidents** and **#support**

This runbook applies to **Hosted API**, **Console**, and **Dedicated control-plane** customer-visible components. For **Private** deployments, see “Dedicated/Private guidance” below.

---

## Roles (who does what)
See: *Incident Comms Roles and Responsibilities* for full definitions.

- **Incident Commander (IC):** owns incident execution and severity, approves scope/impact statements.
- **Comms Lead:** owns status page updates and external-facing wording; ensures TTFU tar...

text:
Operator continuity and customer status playbook

Short description
This playbook standardizes shift continuity, evidence capture, escalation, and customer-facing status messaging for incidents that affect inference availability or quality across Redwood Inference hosted, dedicated, and private deployments. Use this as the single source of truth for handoffs, status automation, and post-incident evidence packaging.

Audience
- On-call engineers, SREs, incident commanders
- Customer Success and support engineers
- Applied ML and runtime engineers involved in mitigation or rollbacks

Scope
- Applies to incidents that impact: API availability, request latency, model correctness, routing/fallback behavior, or system capacity that materially affects customer SLAs.
- Excludes routine maintenance (see maintenance notification SOP).

Primary contacts and ownership
- Document owner_team: engin...

h1. Summary
This page defines the *roles, responsibilities, and approval rules* for customer-facing communications during incidents at Redwood Inference. It standardizes:
* Who owns public updates (Statuspage + customer-facing statements)
* When updates are required and how often they should happen
* How to coordinate with Support/CS so customer responses are consistent
* What content is allowed vs. prohibited (security/legal/customer sensitivity)

This process supports the “Status page transparency upgrades” initiative and is designed to reduce on-call load through templates + automation while improving time-to-first-update (TTFU) and accuracy.

h1. Scope
h2. In scope
* Public Statuspage updates for Hosted API, Dedicated, and (where applicable) Private signals
* Coordination with Support/CS for ticket macros, escalations, and customer outreach
* Approval rules for high-severity incid...

title: Game Day Plan: Hosted Overload Protection (Circuit Breakers + Load Shedding)
preview:
## Summary
This game day validates Redwood Hosted API overload protection behaviors end-to-end: gateway admission control, policy evaluation, smart routing fallback, and serving runtime backpressure. The focus is safe degradation under stress (reject early, stable error semantics, avoid retry storms) while protectin...
text:
## Summary
This game day validates Redwood Hosted API overload protection behaviors end-to-end: gateway admission control, policy evaluation, smart routing fallback, and serving runtime backpressure. The focus is safe degradation under stress (reject early, stable error semantics, avoid retry storms) while protecting tenant isolation and keeping p95/p99 latency within guardrails.

## Objectives
1) Confirm overload protection prevents overload cascades (tail latency + timeouts) during bursty demand.
2) Validate customer-facing error semantics (HTTP mapping, error codes, Retry-After, correlation IDs).
3) Verify dependency brownouts do not amplify into platform-wide incidents (circuit breaker trip + fast fail).
4) Validate regional capacity dips are handled predictably (shed + optional fallback routing) without loops.
5) Exercise oncall operational muscle: detection, triage, mitigations,...

text:
## Purpose
This page defines Redwood Inference’s canonical **public** status page component taxonomy. It is the shared reference for:
- Which components exist on the public status page (names, IDs, grouping).
- What each component means (scope, common failure modes, customer interpretation).
- Ownership (technical owner for mitigation + communications owner for updates).
- **Mapping rules** from internal services/signals to public components using the standard axes: **product area × region × capability**.

This taxonomy is designed to improve customer clarity without exposing customer-specific capacity, topology, or sensitive details.

---

## What this taxonomy is (and is not)
### In scope
- Hosted API components split by **region** and **capability** where failure modes differ (e.g., Streaming vs non-streaming).
- Console components (read-only surfaces that can fail independently fr...

title: Postmortem: 2025-11-07 US-East Streaming Degradation
preview:
## Summary
On 2025-11-07, customers using **streaming responses** in **US-East** experienced elevated disconnects and intermittent upstream errors. Non-streaming requests were largely unaffected.

This incident exposed a major gap in our customer-facing communication: the public status page component coverage was to...
text:
## Summary
On 2025-11-07, customers using **streaming responses** in **US-East** experienced elevated disconnects and intermittent upstream errors. Non-streaming requests were largely unaffected.

This incident exposed a major gap in our customer-facing communication: the public status page component coverage was too coarse ("Hosted API" only), which made it difficult to accurately reflect a streaming-only regional degradation. The resulting delay and ambiguity in external updates directly contributed to customer confusion and escalations.

**Severity:** Sev1

## Customer impact
**Who was affected**
- Customers using streaming (SSE) for Chat Completions via the Hosted API in **us-east-1**.
- Impact was most visible for long-lived streams (longer generations / higher token counts).

**What customers experienced**
- Increased streaming disconnects (client-visible mid-stream termination)...

text:
## Purpose
Validate that the provision-to-ready automation can safely detect common bring-up failures, prevent unsafe nodes from entering serving pools, and provide actionable diagnostics for operators.

This game day intentionally introduces controlled failures for:
- Golden image mismatch
- Driver install failure / driver-toolkit parity drift
- Kubelet join failure
- NCCL smoke test failure

We will evaluate:
- Correct readiness state transitions (Allocated → Provisioning → Validating → Ready | Quarantined)
- Quality of failure reasons + artifacts (logs pointers, step durations)
- Retry/backoff behavior and quarantine policy
- Operational response: triage, remediation, and recovery time

## Scope
In scope:
- Dedicated pools only (staged rollout configuration)
- One region + one GPU type (recommended: us-east / A100) unless explicitly approved for multi-region
- Control-plane readine...

This document defines an orchestration pattern for progressive degradation incidents (partial customer impact, cross-region latency escalations, model-serving fallbacks) and a companion game-day blueprint to validate the operational playbook. - Intent: Give on-call responders a clear, timeboxed set of actions for the first 60 minutes and an exercise plan to validate those actions. This focuses on progressive degradation (increasing latencies, rising p99/p95, slow tail) rather than full hard outages.

title: Customer‑sensitive Notice Lifecycle for Degraded Inference
preview:
Summary
This playbook defines the lifecycle and templates for communicating with high‑sensitivity customers (enterprise, regulated) during inference quality or availability degradations. It standardizes timings, ownership, escalation paths, and message content so notices are predictable, legally defensible, and alig...
text:
Summary
This playbook defines the lifecycle and templates for communicating with high‑sensitivity customers (enterprise, regulated) during inference quality or availability degradations. It standardizes timings, ownership, escalation paths, and message content so notices are predictable, legally defensible, and aligned with SLAs/SLOs.

Scope and definitions
- Applies to incidents affecting: text generation API, chat, embeddings, reranking, or routing logic that can materially affect customer output quality or availability.
- High‑sensitivity customer: an account with contractual notice SLAs, data residency constraints, or regulated workloads.
- Severity tiers used here map to company SLOs:
 - Severity 1 (S1): Total service outage or >50% customer impact on request success for >5 minutes.
 - Severity 2 (S2): Significant degradation (latency >3x P95 or quality regressions for critical r...

title: Postmortem: Latency regression after Optimization Config rollout (rollback required)
preview:
## Summary
On 2025-08-14, a production rollout of an Optimization Config change (batching heuristics + cache toggle for select routes) caused elevated p95/p99 latency for Hosted inference traffic in us-east-1 and eu-west-1. The change was intended to reduce per-token cost via more aggressive continuous batching. Wit...
text:
## Summary
On 2025-08-14, a production rollout of an Optimization Config change (batching heuristics + cache toggle for select routes) caused elevated p95/p99 latency for Hosted inference traffic in us-east-1 and eu-west-1. The change was intended to reduce per-token cost via more aggressive continuous batching. Within ~15 minutes, customer-facing latency breached SLOs and a subset of tenants experienced timeouts.

Mitigation required a manual rollback by re-applying a prior optimization config snapshot, coordinated via on-call and runtime operators. Because the config was not versioned and the apply mechanism was not transactional/idempotent, rollback was slower than desired and required multiple retries to converge worker state.

This incident is the primary driver for the “Safe apply + rollback mechanism” project: introduce immutable config versions, staged rollout with gates, one-...

title: Postmortem: Private VPC egress disruption and region fallback (2025-07-22)
preview:
h2. Summary
On 2025-07-22, a networking change in a customer-controlled VPC (Private deployment) disrupted outbound connectivity to one of the inference subnets. As a mitigation, the customer and Redwood jointly activated *automatic region fallback* to a secondary region for non-residency-bound traffic until routing...
text:
h2. Summary
On 2025-07-22, a networking change in a customer-controlled VPC (Private deployment) disrupted outbound connectivity to one of the inference subnets. As a mitigation, the customer and Redwood jointly activated *automatic region fallback* to a secondary region for non-residency-bound traffic until routing was corrected.

h2. Impact
* Customer: 1 Private deployment (customer-sensitive).
* Symptoms: intermittent timeouts and 502/504 on a subset of inference nodes.
* Duration: ~2 hours of partial impairment; user-visible errors concentrated in the first 35 minutes.

h2. Timeline (UTC)
* 16:20: Customer reports timeouts on chat endpoints.
* 16:27: Redwood on-call engaged; traces show failed egress from subnet private-inf-az2.
* 16:41: Mitigation: enabled *region fallback* for allowed routes to shift traffic from us-east-1 (primary) to us-west-2 (secondary).
* 17:05: Error rate ...

text:
Tabletop Rehearsal Syllabus and Brand-Safe Status / Customer Comms Templates

Overview
This document prescribes a repeatable tabletop rehearsal (non-technical role-play) format for exercising Redwood Inference incident lifecycles, with emphasis on: triage cadence, cross-team escalation, stakeholder communications, and production of evidence for postmortem. It also contains vetted status-page payloads and customer-facing message templates that are brand-safe and legally reviewed.

Scope
- Applicable to incidents that materially impact customer-facing SLOs (latency, availability, embeddings/response accuracy).
- Designed for cross-functional rehearsals (SRE, Serving Runtime, Product, Customer Success, Legal/PR).
- Not a replacement for technical runbooks or live game-day load tests; instead focuses on coordination, comms, and decision-making.

Goals
1. Reduce time-to-acknowledge by prac...

title: Postmortem: Dedicated cluster degradation and fallback to Hosted pool (2025-06-08)
preview:
h2. Summary
On 2025-06-08, a Dedicated GPU pool for a single-tenant customer experienced repeated node flaps and elevated error rates. To maintain availability, we activated an *automatic fallback* to a compatible Hosted pool (same model family) for the affected customer routes until the Dedicated pool stabilized.

...
text:
h2. Summary
On 2025-06-08, a Dedicated GPU pool for a single-tenant customer experienced repeated node flaps and elevated error rates. To maintain availability, we activated an *automatic fallback* to a compatible Hosted pool (same model family) for the affected customer routes until the Dedicated pool stabilized.

h2. Impact
* Customer: 1 Dedicated tenant (customer-approved fallback to Hosted).
* Symptoms: 503 spikes and p95 latency > 6s on tenant routes.
* Duration: ~1 hour degraded performance; ~14 minutes of elevated 503.

h2. Timeline (UTC)
* 14:02: Dedicated pool alert: node health flapping on rw-ded-a100-tenant-17.
* 14:11: Error rate exceeded 2%; on-call paged.
* 14:18: Mitigation: enabled *auto-fallback to Hosted* for tenant routes "chat" and "embeddings".
* 14:23: Errors dropped; latency improved.
* 14:37: Infra drained unstable nodes and replaced 2 GPUs.
* 15:08: Dedicated ...

title: Runbook: Hosted Canary Rollout stuck or traffic split mismatch
preview:
## Purpose
This runbook covers two common incident modes for **Hosted canary rollouts**:

1) **Rollout is stuck** (step not progressing, state machine not advancing, approvals not unblocking, or auto-stop evaluation hanging).
2) **Traffic split mismatch** (observed baseline/canary traffic does not match the configur...
text:
## Purpose
This runbook covers two common incident modes for **Hosted canary rollouts**:

1) **Rollout is stuck** (step not progressing, state machine not advancing, approvals not unblocking, or auto-stop evaluation hanging).
2) **Traffic split mismatch** (observed baseline/canary traffic does not match the configured percentage, or behaves differently by region).

It provides a fast triage path, diagnostic checks, safe mitigations, rollback steps, and escalation guidance.

---

## Scope / systems
**Applies to:** Hosted routes using percentage-based canary split.

**Core components involved:**
- **Console** (customer UI + orchestrator client)
- **Rollout Orchestrator** (control-plane service; rollout state machine)
- **Smart Routing / Data Plane** (enforces split; deterministic cohorting)
- **Policy Engine** (guardrails, approvals, soak-time rules)
- **Telemetry Pipeline** (cohort tag...

Key goals

title: INC-2025-0522 Postmortem: Private deployment capacity drop from GPU fragmentation
preview:
# Summary
On 2025-05-22, a Private (VPC) deployment experienced a 35–45% effective throughput drop and periodic timeouts. The fleet had enough raw GPUs, but fragmentation and placement constraints reduced usable capacity for the target model group.

**Incident ID:** INC-2025-0522
**Customer impact:** Single private ...
text:
# Summary
On 2025-05-22, a Private (VPC) deployment experienced a 35–45% effective throughput drop and periodic timeouts. The fleet had enough raw GPUs, but fragmentation and placement constraints reduced usable capacity for the target model group.

**Incident ID:** INC-2025-0522
**Customer impact:** Single private customer (enterprise), production
**Owning engineering team:** Eng Infra

# What happened
A model group was pinned to a specific GPU SKU and memory profile. After an unrelated maintenance rotation, the pool contained a mix of GPU SKUs and partially utilized nodes. The scheduler could not place new replicas that met the constraints, leading to queue growth and timeouts.

# Root cause
**Primary:** Fragmentation of allocatable GPU resources under strict placement constraints.
**Contributing factors:**
- Lack of a fragmentation metric and alert
- Runbook did not include defragm...

title: Dedicated Burst Capacity: Game Day Plan (Guardrails, Telemetry Lag, Routing Failures, Rollback Drills)

This game day validates that **Dedicated Burst Capacity** guardrails protect non-bursting tenants and platform SLOs under stress conditions that are hard to reproduce organically.

The plan covers:
- Synthetic bursts (single tenant and multi-tenant)

text:
## Purpose
This game day validates that **Dedicated Burst Capacity** guardrails protect non-bursting tenants and platform SLOs under stress conditions that are hard to reproduce organically.

The plan covers:
- Synthetic bursts (single tenant and multi-tenant)
- Admission control under pool saturation
- Fairness and anti-starvation behavior
- Induced **telemetry lag** and stale capacity signals
- Routing failures (timeouts, partial dependency outages)
- Rollback drills (feature flags, global circuit breaker, per-region/hardware allow-list)

Success criteria and rollback triggers are explicitly defined so on-call can make fast decisions.

---

## Scope
### In scope
- Burst pool(s) in a single region/hardware class (primary), and a secondary region/hardware class (sanity)
- Admission controller behavior and denial reason codes
- Routing integration (admission decision consumption, backp...

text:
## Purpose
This playbook standardizes how on-call responders use Incident Bot v2 diagnostics in the first 5 minutes of an incident. The goal is to reduce time-to-triage and time-to-mitigation (MTTR) by quickly answering three questions:
1) Did we just deploy something?
2) Did config/routing/policy change?
3) Are we saturated (GPU/queue/KV cache/capacity) and should we degrade?

Incident Bot v2 posts a set of Slack “context cards” into #incidents and #eng-oncall threads. Cards are designed to be safe-by-default (tenant-safe, redacted), bounded-cost (rate limited + cached), and resilient (degraded mode under partial outages).

---

## When to use this
Use this playbook for:
- Any Sev0/Sev1, and any Sev2 where symptoms suggest deploy regression, config change, or regional saturation.
- Common symptom patterns: 503 spikes, p99 latency regression, streaming disconnects/timeouts, sudden thr...

title: Runbook: Recent deploys + config diff debugging (Incident Bot v2)
preview:
## Purpose
This runbook explains how to interpret the Incident Bot v2 **Recent deploys** and **Config diff** context cards during the first 5–15 minutes of an incident. It also covers common pitfalls (staged rollouts, partial propagation, config layering) and manual fallback commands when bot context is missing or s...
text:
## Purpose
This runbook explains how to interpret the Incident Bot v2 **Recent deploys** and **Config diff** context cards during the first 5–15 minutes of an incident. It also covers common pitfalls (staged rollouts, partial propagation, config layering) and manual fallback commands when bot context is missing or suspicious.

**Use this when:**
- Errors/latency spike shortly after a deploy or config push
- Symptoms vary by region, pool, or tenant
- The bot shows a config diff you don’t fully trust (redaction, partial propagation, multiple layers)

**Do not use this for:**
- GPU saturation / queueing root-cause analysis (use the GPU saturation triage runbook)
- End-to-end incident process (use the incident process playbook)

---

## Quick workflow (first 5 minutes)
1) Identify **scope**: service/route, region, deployment mode (Hosted / Dedicated / Private), and at least one affected t...

title: INC-2025-0303 Postmortem: Dedicated autoscaler lag + quota ceiling caused sustained throttling
preview:
# Summary
On 2025-03-03, a set of Dedicated customers saw elevated 429 throttling and increased queueing latency for ~62 minutes. The dedicated autoscaler attempted to add GPU nodes but hit a cloud quota ceiling, while the fallback routing policy was configured to prefer in-region capacity.

**Incident ID:** INC-202...
text:
# Summary
On 2025-03-03, a set of Dedicated customers saw elevated 429 throttling and increased queueing latency for ~62 minutes. The dedicated autoscaler attempted to add GPU nodes but hit a cloud quota ceiling, while the fallback routing policy was configured to prefer in-region capacity.

**Incident ID:** INC-2025-0303
**Customer impact:** Dedicated tier (subset), primarily eu-west
**Owning engineering team:** Eng SRE

# What happened
A scheduled load test by a customer coincided with a new prompt pattern increasing average output length. The autoscaler hit scale limits and then failed to provision due to quota. The system kept retrying, causing prolonged throttling.

# Timeline (UTC)
- 2025-03-03 09:12: Alert: dedicated-throttle-rate > 2%

text:
h2. Summary
On 2025-03-03, a GPU capacity shortfall in eu-west-1 impacted a subset of Hosted and Dedicated traffic pinned to that region. Mitigation required activating *automatic region fallback* (eu-west-1 -> us-east-1) for eligible routes until additional capacity was brought online.

h2. Impact
* Elevated p95 latency and increased 429/503 responses for region-pinned routes in eu-west-1.
* Customers: 7 Hosted orgs and 1 Dedicated tenant with optional cross-region allowance enabled.
* Duration: ~41 minutes of degraded performance; ~18 minutes of hard errors for the most constrained pool.

h2. Timeline (UTC)
* 09:42: Capacity alert: available_gpu_count < 5 in eu-west-1 pool rw-a100-eu-01.
* 09:48: Saturation exceeded 95% and queue wait climbed > 400ms.
* 09:51: Mitigation: enabled *region fallback* for "chat-default" and "embeddings-default" where customer policy allows cross-region....

text:
h1. Purpose
This runbook covers production incidents where *Hosted API data residency enforcement* incorrectly blocks legitimate traffic ("false positives"). It provides:

* Triage and verification steps
* Immediate mitigation options (including feature flag overrides) with safety constraints
* Customer communication guidance
* Rollback procedure and post-incident follow-ups

This runbook applies to the residency enforcement path across:

* API Gateway (request validation + error contract)
* Smart Routing (region selection + fallback restrictions)
* Residency Policy Service (control plane policy evaluation)
* Observability/audit logging related to enforcement decisions

h1. Symptoms & Detection
h2. Common symptoms
* Sudden spike in blocked requests for one or more regions, orgs, or projects.
* Customers report requests failing after enabling region pinning.
* Support reports inconsist...

title: Postmortem: 2025-02-07 Hosted API elevated 503s due to deploy regression
preview:
## Summary
On 2025-02-07, Redwood Inference’s Hosted API experienced elevated HTTP 503 responses following a production deploy of the **inference-router** service (request admission + routing layer). A regression in the new release increased the probability of transient upstream timeout handling being converted into...
text:
## Summary
On 2025-02-07, Redwood Inference’s Hosted API experienced elevated HTTP 503 responses following a production deploy of the **inference-router** service (request admission + routing layer). A regression in the new release increased the probability of transient upstream timeout handling being converted into immediate 503s under certain concurrency patterns.

The incident was resolved by halting the rollout and rolling back to the prior stable version. The incident highlighted a major operational gap: responders did not have **reliable “recent deploy context” in the first five minutes**, which materially increased time-to-triage.

This postmortem is one of the primary justifications for the **Incident bot + diagnostics improvements (Incident Bot v2)** initiative.

---

## Customer impact
**Customer-visible symptoms**
- Elevated 503 responses from Hosted API endpoints (chat/com...

h1. Customer impact
- Duration of elevated errors: ~32 mi...
text:
h1. Summary
On 2025-02-19, the Hosted API in us-east-1 experienced a regional degradation that caused elevated 5xx errors and high latency for chat completions. We mitigated by failing over production traffic to us-west-2 using GSLB weights via `trafficctl`.

h1. Customer impact
- Duration of elevated errors: ~32 minutes
- Primary symptoms: HTTP 5xx spikes, increased p95 latency, some transient streaming disconnects during cutover
- Affected surfaces: /v1/chat.completions primarily; embeddings had smaller impact

h1. Timeline (UTC)
- 17:10 Incident declared P0; IC assigned
- 17:14 Decision: initiate failover to us-west-2
- 17:16 TTL lowered to 60
- 17:18 Weights set to 90/10
- 17:23 Weights set to 50/50
- 17:28 Weights set to 0/100 (full failover)
- 17:41 Error rate returns to baseline; monitoring continues

h1. What worked
- Having `trafficctl` commands copy/pasted into the incident ...

title: Postmortem: 2025-02-17 Sev1 — Tensor-parallel decode hang causing Dedicated timeouts
preview:
## Executive summary
On 2025-02-17, a Dedicated customer experienced elevated request timeouts and streaming stalls due to a tensor-parallel (TP) decode hang in the serving runtime on A100-80GB multi-GPU replicas.

A subset of TP replicas entered a state where one or more ranks stopped making forward progress in the...
text:
## Executive summary
On 2025-02-17, a Dedicated customer experienced elevated request timeouts and streaming stalls due to a tensor-parallel (TP) decode hang in the serving runtime on A100-80GB multi-GPU replicas.

A subset of TP replicas entered a state where one or more ranks stopped making forward progress in the decode loop while other ranks continued to enqueue work, resulting in blocked NCCL collectives and wedged decode steps. This manifested as requests that never completed (hangs), triggering downstream timeouts and cascading retries.

Immediate mitigations (replica drain/recycle and temporary concurrency caps) restored service while we collected logs and narrowed the most likely failure mode. This incident materially accelerated the “Multi-GPU (TP) stability fixes” program (ENG-4821).

## Customer impact
**Primary impacted customer:** Acme AI (Dedicated, A100-80GB, TP=4 per ...
links: Linear: ENG-4821 Multi-GPU (TP) stability fixes initiative
Linear: ENG-4823 Add TP heartbeat metrics and stall detectors
Linear: ENG-4824 Scheduler fix — collective ordering starvation
Linear: ENG-4825 NCCL stream sync hardening
Linear: ENG-4826 TP watchdog timeout and safe abort
Linear: ENG-4828 Perf-canary TP stress scenarios
Linear: ENG-4829 Release plan — runtime 1.19 TP stability
Jira: SUP-48321 Tensor parallel hang — Dedicated (A100)
Jira: SUP-48344 Streaming timeouts during peak load (TP)

## Incident summary
**Incident ID:** IR-2025-017
**Start:** 2025-02-11
**End:** 2025-02-14
**Severity:** Sev2

Some Redwood Private tenants experienced **dropped audit log events** in the audit-event-router when the downstream sink became slow or intermittently unavailable. This caused **incomplete audit exports** for specific time windows.

## Impact
### Customer impact (confirmed)
The following customer accounts reported missing audit events and were validated by Redwood Support/Engineering:
- HelioPay
- AcmeBank
- Globex
- Umbrella Bio

### Customer impact (unconfirmed)
- Northwind Health (reported informally; pending concrete timestamps at time of writing)

### What was affected
- Audit events related to admin actions (RBAC role bindings, API key lifecycle)
- Export paths using HTTP push sinks were overrepresented, but the core issue was router backpressure handling

## Detection
...

text:
h2. Summary
On 2025-02-11, the Hosted API experienced elevated p95 latency on /v1/chat/completions for ~23 minutes. Mitigation included activating an automatic model fallback policy to keep requests flowing while we reduced load on the primary model pool.

h2. Impact
* Customer-facing: increased latency, intermittent timeouts, elevated 5xx for some bursts.
* Affected product: Redwood API (Hosted).
* Affected tiers: primarily Free and Growth tiers on the default route.

h2. Timeline (UTC)
* 17:06: Alert fired: Chat latency p95 > 4.0s (threshold 2.0s) for 5 min.
* 17:11: On-call confirmed queueing on primary model pool (rw-llama-3.1-70b-instruct).
* 17:14: Mitigation: enabled *automatic model fallback* on the router policy for the default route.
* 17:16: Router began automatically rerouting overflow traffic to rw-llama-3.1-8b-instruct (fallback target) when saturation exceeded 85%.
* 17...

title: Private DR Game Day Plan (Backup/Restore Toolkit Rehearsal)
preview:
## Summary
This game day validates Redwood Private Day-2 operations for backup/restore and disaster recovery (DR) in a controlled, non-production environment. The rehearsal is designed to be repeatable by SRE/Solutions teams and to produce concrete follow-up actions (runbook fixes, tooling gaps, alert tuning, and re...
text:
## Summary
This game day validates Redwood Private Day-2 operations for backup/restore and disaster recovery (DR) in a controlled, non-production environment. The rehearsal is designed to be repeatable by SRE/Solutions teams and to produce concrete follow-up actions (runbook fixes, tooling gaps, alert tuning, and release readiness).

**Primary outcome:** demonstrate a deterministic restore of Redwood Private control-plane state into a clean Kubernetes cluster using the supported toolkit and runbooks, then pass post-restore validation (health + functional smoke tests) within agreed RTO.

---

## Goals and non-goals
### Goals
1. Validate **end-to-end control-plane restore** from an encrypted backup artifact using the supported `restore apply` workflow and documented bootstrap order.
2. Validate **RPO/RTO expectations** for the chosen archetype (VPC single-region reference environment) a...

preview:
# Goal

# Decision criteria (any may qualify)
- Customer-visible impact is ongoing (e.g., 5xx or severe latency)

text:
# Goal
Provide a consistent checklist for oncall leads when considering and executing a manual regional traffic shift (failover) for production inference traffic.

# Decision criteria (any may qualify)
- Customer-visible impact is ongoing (e.g., 5xx or severe latency)
- Cloud provider incident or internal outage affecting a region
- GPU capacity shortfall that cannot be recovered within the next 30 minutes

# Pre-flight
- Confirm incident commander and assign roles (SRE lead, Platform routing, Infra capacity)
- Identify impacted surface (chat, embeddings, rerank)
- Confirm target region/cluster headroom with Eng Infra
- Prepare comms: status page update + internal broadcast

# Execution steps
1) Announce intent
 - Post in #incidents: "Planning traffic shift from <regionA> to <regionB> for <surface>"

2) Apply routing override
 - Follow: confluence://eng-platform/router-policy-manual-t...

text:
Summary:
This document consolidates our incident mobilization lifecycle, concise role checklists for responders, and a curated archive of status / customer communication templates. It is intended for oncall responders, bridge operators, SRE leads, and engineering managers who participate in incident activation or customer-facing communications.

Scope:
- Applies to all service-impacting incidents affecting hosted API, Dedicated, or Private control plane surface areas.
- Focuses on rapid mobilization (first 0–60 minutes) and stabilization 60–240 minutes.
- Not a replacement for individual runbooks — this is a lightweight orchestration layer and template set.

When to mobilize (decision criteria):
1) Automatic triggers:
 - SLO breach: 99th percentile latency > 2x target for 10m or error rate > 1% for 5m (see SLO matrix).
 - PagerDuty alert severity = P0 or P1 (automated).
2) Human trigg...

preview:
# Summary
On 2025-01-12, Redwood Hosted API traffic in us-east experienced elevated 429/503 responses and increased p95 latency due to GPU pool capacity exhaustion in the shared A100-80GB pool (fleet: a100-us-east-1).

**Incident ID:** INC-2025-0112
**Customer impact:** Broad (self_serve + enterprise workloads route...
text:
# Summary
On 2025-01-12, Redwood Hosted API traffic in us-east experienced elevated 429/503 responses and increased p95 latency due to GPU pool capacity exhaustion in the shared A100-80GB pool (fleet: a100-us-east-1).

**Incident ID:** INC-2025-0112
**Customer impact:** Broad (self_serve + enterprise workloads routed to us-east)
**Owning engineering team:** Eng Infra (GPU fleet & capacity)

# What happened
A sustained burst in long-context chat workloads coincided with a delayed capacity scale-up. The scheduler began queuing requests beyond safe limits. Backpressure increased, and the API gateway started returning 429s. When queue timeouts were exceeded, the runtime returned 503s.

# Timeline (UTC)
- 2025-01-12 18:06: Alerts: api-gateway 429 rate > 3% for 5m
- 18:10: On-call engaged (Eng Infra)
- 18:18: Confirmed fleet utilization pinned (GPU alloc > 98%, queue depth rising)
- 18:25: ...

title: Planned Maintenance and Change Communications
preview:
h1. Summary

h1. Scope
Applies to production changes for Hosted API and Console....
text:
h1. Summary
This page defines RedwoodInferences requirements for communicating planned maintenance and production changes that may impact customers. It also describes internal communication expectations for changes with no expected customer impact.

h1. Scope
Applies to production changes for Hosted API and Console. For Private deployments, coordinate with Customer Success and follow the customer-specific comms plan.

h1. When customer communications are required
Customer communications are required if any of the following are true:
* Any expected downtime, degraded performance, or increased error rates
* Any planned breaking change to APIs, SDK behavior, auth, rate limits, or quotas
* Any change that may cause delayed/duplicate billing events or usage reporting gaps
* Any region-level maintenance that reduces redundancy

h1. Lead time requirements
* Minor maintenance (no downtime exp...

text:
Summary
-------
This runbook defines the incident command structure, escalation matrix, triage playbooks, on-call rotation norms, game day checklist, and customer/status communications templates for production incidents that affect Redwood Inference hosted and dedicated inference services. It is written for responders (SRE, Platform, Serving Runtime, and Product engineers) and on-call leads.

Purpose
-------
- Provide a single-source-of-truth for running incidents from detection to postmortem.
- Ensure incidents are handled predictably with clear ownership, SLA-aware decisions, and consistent external comms.
- Reduce time-to-detect and time-to-recover by standardizing diagnostic steps and escalation paths.

Scope
-----
This document covers:
- Severity definition and SLO thresholds for public-facing inference endpoints (hosted API and dedicated endpoints).
- Incident command roles & re...

title: Ops continuity: runbook library, rotation cadence, and customer comms templates
preview:
Purpose

The goa...
text:
Purpose
------
This page centralizes the Ops continuity artifacts you need to run, measure, and communicate during incidents: a runbook library index, standard rotation cadence and handoff checklist, game day scenarios and evaluation criteria, and ready-to-send status page & customer communication templates. The goal is predictable, low-latency incident response with clear ownership and consistent customer experience.

Scope & audience
----------------
- Audience: on-call engineers, SREs, incident commanders (ICs), engineering managers, support/CS for customer comms.
- Scope: production incidents affecting the Redwood inference platform (hosted API, dedicated, private control plane). Non-production incidents (staging/dev) use lightweight on-call procedures (see eng-sre/team-wiki).

Quick links

- Runbook library index (below)
- Rotation cadence & handoff checklist (section)...
